evals.prompt: Decompose this feature for the test-benchmark project: 'Add user authentication with OAuth2 (Google, GitHub) including login page, session management, and role-based access control'. The dev config is at .ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/dev-config.yml. Project conventions are in .ai/projects/test-benchmark/knowledge/conventions.yml. Design component specs exist at .ai/projects/test-benchmark/design/tokens/. Write the decomposition to .ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/feature-decomposition-test.yml.
evals.expected_output: A feature decomposition with multiple components, each having name, description, type, boundaries, and files_affected. Frontend components should have designer specs.
expectations: Identifies at least 3 distinct components (auth provider setup, login UI, session management are the minimum)
Each component has a type field (backend, frontend, or fullstack)
Each component has files_affected with specific file paths
Frontend components include designer_specs or visual specifications

evals.prompt: Decompose: 'Add real-time notifications with WebSocket — bell icon in header, notification dropdown, mark-as-read, and push notification opt-in'. Dev config: .ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/dev-config.yml. Consider that this is a Next.js TypeScript project. Write output to .ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/notifications-decomposition.yml.
evals.expected_output: Component breakdown separating WebSocket server, notification UI, notification storage, and push notification subsystems.
expectations: Separates backend (WebSocket server, notification storage) from frontend (bell icon, dropdown) components
Identifies component boundaries — which files/directories each component owns
Includes a scope validation or PO validation section

evals.prompt: Decompose: 'Add search functionality with full-text search, filters by category/date/author, pagination, and saved searches'. The project uses Next.js with TypeScript (dev config at .ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/dev-config.yml). Consider existing patterns in the project. Output to .ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/search-decomposition.yml.
evals.expected_output: Component breakdown for search with clear separation between search backend, filter UI, pagination, and saved searches persistence.
expectations: Identifies search backend/API as a separate component from the search UI
Includes filter components with their specific boundaries
Each component lists concrete file paths in files_affected
